target.title: fix(diagnostics): wire memoryPressureThresholds config to diagnostic memory monitor
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` accepts a `thresholds` parameter but no caller passes one, so the four hardcoded defaults (RSS warn 1.5GB/crit 3GB, heap warn 1GB/crit 2GB) cannot be tuned without patching the dist bundle.
- Solution: Add `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds` config fields and wire them through the heartbeat to `emitDiagnosticMemorySample`.
- What changed: Added `rssWarningBytes`, `rssCriticalBytes`, `heapUsedWarningBytes`, `heapUsedCriticalBytes` to the diagnostics config schema, types, help, labels, reload plan, and the heartbeat caller.
- What did NOT change: The default values and resolver logic in `diagnostic-memory.ts` are unchanged; the new config is entirely optional and defaults to existing behavior.

Fixes #87441

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Users cannot override memory pressure thresholds through config; the four thresholds are hardcoded in `resolveThresholds` defaults.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v22, OpenClaw worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87551`. Gateway started on port 19002 with `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds.rssWarningBytes: 1024` (1KB — deliberately low to trigger pressure immediately on startup).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87551
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/ocp87551-gw \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19002 \
  OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://api.deepseek.com \
  pnpm gateway:dev
  # Config: {"diagnostics":{"memoryPressureThresholds":{"rssWarningBytes":1024}}}
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
[gateway] loading configuration…
[gateway] starting...
[gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: ..., 11.1s)
[gateway] ready
[heartbeat] started
[diagnostics/memory] memory pressure: level=warning reason=rss_threshold
  rssBytes=761544704 heapUsedBytes=582034584 thresholdBytes=1024
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway started successfully with `rssWarningBytes=1024`. Within ~30s of startup, the heartbeat emitted a `diagnostic.memory.pressure` event with `thresholdBytes=1024` — exactly the custom value, NOT the hardcoded default of 1610612736 (1.5GB). This proves the config is wired through to `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` and the custom threshold controls when pressure events fire.
- **What was not tested:** Did not test sustained memory load to trigger the critical threshold. The warning-level event with a deliberately low threshold is sufficient to prove end-to-end config wiring.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/logging/diagnostic-memory.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: RSS warning threshold override and heap warning threshold override each produce the correct `diagnostic.memory.pressure` event.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact boundary where thresholds are applied before any diagnostic event is emitted.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The heartbeat in `diagnostic.ts` called `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` without passing `thresholds`, and no config fields existed to populate them.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No config schema fields for memory pressure thresholds, and no test coverage for heap threshold overrides.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
